transcript.text: Warren Buffett has $381 billion in cash. That's more money than the whole country of Greece makes. But he's not spending it. For nearly three years, he's been selling. Even as the S&P 500 went up 80%. So while everyone else is buying, he's waiting. Why? Because he's done this before. In 1999 and 2007, he waited, then bought big when prices crashed. And now, the market is at 230% of the US economy. That's very high. So he's sitting on cash, waiting. The real question is, do prices fall first or does he start buying? Editors, projectfile on earnededits.com. Visit now and download it.
